responses.text: The target article from news.com.au claims that NASA scientists have discovered evidence of a parallel universe where time runs backwards based on particle detections in Antarctica.
This viral claim is false and fundamentally misrepresents the results of an actual scientific experiment.

The Antarctic Impulsive Transient Antenna (ANITA) project, a high-altitude balloon experiment partially funded by NASA, did detect unusual radio signals that appeared to show high-energy particles moving upward out of the ice.
While these upward-moving signals are difficult to explain using the standard model of physics, the researchers involved did not conclude they came from an alternate reality.
Peter Gorham, the principal investigator for ANITA, explicitly denied the viral narrative, stating that his team never wrote about or hypothesized parallel universes to explain their data.

The parallel universe rumor actually originated from an April 2020 feature in New Scientist magazine, which speculated about a separate theoretical paper proposing a hypothetical "CPT symmetric universe."
Tabloids and online news outlets distorted this theoretical discussion into a false assertion that NASA scientists had found definitive proof of a reverse-time universe.
Other physicists have since proposed more mundane explanations for the anomaly, such as subsurface reflections of cosmic rays bouncing off unique layers of compacted snow known as firn.
